title: WebUIActionRequest
description: Schema for a WebUI control-plane action request. Captures the intended action, its mode (preview or execute), risk level, and safety guards. All mutating actions default to preview mode; execute mode requires explicit allowlists and reason. See docs/ai-native/webui-action-contract.md for policy and field-level documentation.

requestId.description: Client-generated idempotency key. Prevents duplicate action dispatch on retry.

mode.description: preview = compute effects without applying. execute = apply the action. Mutating actions default to preview; execute requires explicit allowlist and reason.

actionType.description: The control-plane action to perform. Each type has a defined risk profile and required parameters.

riskLevel.description: Risk classification of this action. low = read-only or auto-safe. medium = reversible with side effects. high = destructive or hard to reverse. critical = bypasses safety gates. The WebUI must show escalating confirmation for medium+ actions.

humanRequired.description: When true, the action cannot be dispatched by automated agents — a human must initiate or confirm. Required for high/critical risk actions.

targetIssue.description: GitHub issue number this action targets. Required for worker-related actions.

targetPR.description: GitHub PR number this action targets. Required for merge-related actions.

targetProviderId.description: Provider identifier for provider-related actions (enable, disable, adjust concurrency).

allowlist.description: Explicit target allowlist for execute mode. Limits the blast radius of the action to the listed targets. Required when mode is execute.

reason.description: Human-readable justification for the action. Required when mode is execute or riskLevel is medium or higher.

params.description: Action-type-specific parameters. Shape depends on actionType.

force.description: Force flag for actions that support it (e.g. force-merge-pr bypasses CI checks).

comment.description: Optional comment to attach to the resulting artifact (PR comment, issue comment, audit trail).

requestedBy.description: Identity of the requester (e.g. 'human:login', 'agent:worker-id'). Null if unknown.

requestedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the action was requested.
